_comment: REAL semantic-eval SUBSET fixture for the PixelRAG darwin harness (ADR-264). Each query is a PARAPHRASE that shares MEANING but few exact words with its same-topic tiles, so keyword overlap alone cannot solve it and recall@10 / NDCG@10 stay below 1.0 for a non-semantic ranker. Schema is harness-native: an object with a 'queries' array of {query_id, text}; 'image' paths are placeholders (M0 text-only). 12 queries, 2 per topic, across 6 topics. Still tiny vs the 8.28M-page upstream corpus.

queries.text: How do leaves turn daylight into stored fuel and give off breathable gas?
Which cellular process locks atmospheric carbon into sugar molecules?
Why did the people of 18th-century Paris overthrow their king and end royal rule?
What happened during the bloody period of mass executions after the throne fell?
What is left behind when a giant star dies and traps everything including light?
How do scientists weigh the unseen monster lurking at a galaxy's center?
What makes a tiny, strong café drink with a golden crema on top?
How does adjusting how fine the beans are ground change the taste of a pulled shot?
How does a message travel across the internet split into addressed chunks?
What mechanism makes sure transferred data shows up intact and in order?
What musical style around 1700 used ornate, dramatic writing over a continuous bass?
How are several chasing melodic lines woven together in a fugue from that age?
